displayTitle: Down the Centerline: Bar and Rod

beats.narration: Bar and rod are the ingot squeezed into one dimension — almost everything important inside ends up long, thin, and parallel to the axis.
Forming draws the ingot's leftovers into lines: pipe becomes a centerline thread, inclusions become stringers, segregation becomes a dirty core.

payload.heading: Drawn out at or near the centerline

beats.narration: Long reflectors sit broadside to a radial beam. On a 100 millimeter bar, the centerline echo lands near seventeen microseconds, the far wall near thirty-four.

payload.heading: Radial scan: centerline broadside
payload.note: CENTERLINE ECHO ≈17 µs · FAR WALL ≈34 µs

beats.narration: Bursts and chevron cracks lie roughly transverse — a radial beam meets them edge-on. Catch them from the bar end, or with an angle beam.

chips.t: Straight beam from the bar end
Or angle beam along the axis

beats.narration: So here's the rule: picture the flaw, hit it broadside, and never sign off bar stock on one beam direction alone.

payload.sub: Orientation thinking — the bar and rod skill.
